message:
bench(storage): env-driven group skipping and configurable size lists

Adds two ergonomic knobs to benches/storage_perf.rs so iterating on
specific benchmarks during perf work doesn't require rebuilding with
a different filter every time.

- New `should_skip_group(name)` helper. Each `bench_*` function
  short-circuits when `BENCH_ONLY_GROUP` is set to a value other than
  the current group's name. This lets `BENCH_ONLY_GROUP=storage/sync`
  cargo bench keep Criterion's machinery for that one group while
  silently returning from the others. Wired into all 18+ existing
  groups (create, create_batch, update, close, delete, list, list_filtered, ...).
- New `configured_sizes(defaults, env_var)` helper. Parses a
  comma-separated list of usize values from the named env var and
  falls back to `defaults` if the var is unset, malformed, or yields
  an empty list. Currently consumed by the sync-size loops via
  `BENCH_SYNC_SIZES` so a focused run can do
  `BENCH_SYNC_SIZES=100,500 cargo bench` without editing the source.

The defaults are unchanged, so a plain `cargo bench` produces the
same result it did before.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
